title: Bracteocarpus papuanus
intro: Bracteocarpus papuanus, also known as Papua bracteocarpus, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Bracteocarpus papuanus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bracteocarpus papuanus is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and musculoskeletal systems.

The genus Bracteocarpus is a small group of plants that are mostly found in tropical regions of the world.
